headline: Migrant domestic workers excluded from Employment Act protections
context: Despite 2022 Employment Act amendments, an estimated 340,000 migrant domestic workers remain excluded from key labour protections in Malaysia.

cards.big: 340,000 migrant domestic workers excluded from Employment Act key provisions
cards.sub: No minimum wage, working hour limits, or rest day requirements apply

cards.big: First Schedule of Employment Act 1955 exempts domestic workers from most provisions
cards.sub: 2022 amendments expanded coverage but maintained domestic worker exemptions

cards.big: ILO Convention 189 on Domestic Workers not ratified by Malaysia
cards.sub: Convention guarantees domestic workers equal treatment with other workers

cards.big: Tenaganita documented 890 abuse cases involving domestic workers in 2024
cards.sub: Isolated working conditions in private homes make detection difficult

cards.big: Exempting 340,000 workers from labour law is legalised exploitation
Indonesia temporarily suspended domestic worker deployment to Malaysia in 2023
